Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Linear for all ExpenseBot project tickets using linear_list_issues (filter by project "Expense Auto-Categorization Engine" or project_id "proj_expense_autocategorizer"). Discovers 15+ tickets spanning ENG-201 through ENG-220, with various statuses, assignees, and priorities.
- Check Linear comments on key remediation tickets using linear_list_comments (for issues: ENG-214 Vectral config fix, ENG-215 Mosaic config fix, ENG-217 pipeline hardening, ENG-216 audit, ENG-220 duplicate detection, ENG-219 launch readiness checklist). Discovers progress notes, blockers, and completion signals not reflected in ticket statuses. Specifically finds comment_lena_deploy_verified on ENG-215 stating "Config corrections have been deployed and confirmed in production" and "Closing ENG-214. Now starting on pipeline hardening (ENG-217)," indicating ENG-214 and ENG-215 are effectively done despite still showing in_progress. Also finds Dmitri's audit results (comment_dmitri_audit_results on ENG-217) with the full breakdown of 47 expenses, and comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context on ENG-220 where Dmitri states he will begin Layer 1 hash dedup implementation and will work with Lena on ingestion pipeline changes.
- Search Slack #engineering channel for ExpenseBot discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or "relaunch" or similar). Discovers Samira's remediation plan (Mar 18), Dmitri's full audit results (47 expenses, 83% accuracy, $885 exposure), Lena's config fixes confirmed deployed, Dmitri's stipend duplicate findings, and Lena's note about pushing back ENG-187 (invoice template safety checks).
- Search emails for ExpenseBot-related discussions using search_emails (query: "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or "ExpenseBot" or "pilot" or similar). Discovers Elena's pause directive email (March 14, subject "Immediate: Pause ExpenseBot auto-decisions"), Samira's remediation plan email (March 16, cc'd to engineering leads), the audit summary email from Dmitri detailing $885 exposure across 8 affected expenses, Dmitri's fraud escalation to Marcus about three suspicious stipend submissions (rec_stipend_022, rec_stipend_023, rec_stipend_024) with QuickBooks holds recommended, and Marcus's reply confirming QuickBooks holds placed and Mina/Emeka assigned for client-side verification.
- Check Airtable stipend records for Vectral Systems using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (table: "Stipend Transactions", filter for Vectral). Key findings: (a) Danielle Osei $475 chair, correctly "Approved" (Mina fixed), (b) Kevin Tran $185 internet, correctly "Approved" (Mina fixed), (c) Maria Santos $550 standing desk, still "Pending Approval" (NOT corrected), (d) James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, still "Rejected" or "Pending Approval" (NOT corrected), (e) Lisa Park $130 wifi, still "Pending Approval" (NOT corrected), (f) Kevin Tran $895 ergonomic chair (April 23), "Approved" but Vectral limit is $600, over-limit approval during manual review.
- Check Airtable stipend records for Mosaic Robotics using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (table: "Stipend Transactions", filter for Mosaic). Finds Andre Washington $1,200 desk (false approval, absorbed by MoveOps), Andre Washington $350 coworking (false approval, absorbed), Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes (third false approval found during audit).
- Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or similar). Discovers samira.tariq@moveops.com.
- Find the #engineering Slack channel ID using channels_list. Discovers the channel ID for posting the status summary.
- Update stale Linear tickets using linear_update_issue. Update tickets where Slack/email confirms work is done but Linear status is stale (e.g., ENG-214 and ENG-215 confirmed deployed in Slack on Mar 18 but may still show "in_progress").
- Add comments to tickets with blocking issues using linear_create_comment. Comment on ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist still in todo), ENG-220 (Dmitri's Layer 1 implementation depends on Lena's ENG-217 pipeline work), and ENG-187 (invoice safety, Samira flagged as critical after second incident).
- Post status summary in #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message. Summary includes per-ticket status breakdown, three uncorrected false rejections in Airtable, Kevin Tran over-limit approval, ENG-220 dependent on ENG-217 completion, ENG-219 not started, and overall relaunch readiness assessment (NOT READY).
- Send readiness report email to Samira using send_email (to: samira.tariq@moveops.com, from: lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com). Email contains full readiness assessment with per-ticket status, identified blockers, Airtable correction gap (3 of 5 Vectral false rejections still uncorrected), manual review quality issue (Kevin Tran over-limit approval), fraud hold status from Marcus's email, and recommendation that relaunch is not ready.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or linear_list_projects) to retrieve ExpenseBot-related tickets.
tool selection
